categories.name: CREATE_ENTRY
categories.precondition: App launched and on a list/dashboard view that exposes a creation affordance (e.g. floating action button or equivalent primary create control); target app is installed and reachable from the launcher
abstract_steps: Launch the target app from launcher/app list
Navigate to the list view where new items are created
Trigger the create affordance (primary create control, commonly a floating + button) and wait for the entry form to appear
If a type toggle is present, choose the correct type (e.g. one of the mutually exclusive categories offered)
Set required fields in order: title/name, amount/value, date (via date picker if needed), classification (category/label), note/description
Adjust date/time via pickers if specified
When modifying a pre-filled field, clear existing text (select-all then remove, or use backspace) before typing
Confirm/save via the primary save action
Return to a list/summary view and verify the new item appears with the correct values
failure_modes: Repeated taps on create button without detecting whether the entry form actually opened
Typing into a field without clearing pre-existing text, leading to concatenated values
Tapping the wrong sub-area of a row and opening a secondary dialog instead of the entry form
Declaring task complete based on an unrelated pre-existing entry
Required numeric field left blank causing validation error
Wrong app launched due to ambiguous icon/name
Picker dialog closed without confirming, losing the selection
verification_checklist: Entry form was observed (not just list) before entering data
All target fields show the intended final values before saving
Post-save list contains a new entry with matching amount/date/type/note
Displayed values match requested name/amount/category/date/time/note
Aggregate totals updated accordingly

categories.name: MODIFY_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Target entry exists in the app's data store and is visible in a list; its detail form is accessible via tapping the row
abstract_steps: Locate target item by date/amount/category via list/search
Open its detail form by tapping the text/amount region of the row (avoid icon/label area that may open a picker)
Enter edit mode
Clear existing field content before typing (select-all or long-press backspace) and replace each target field (amount, date, type, note, label, classification)
Use the date picker for date changes and confirm with the positive action
Save with the primary confirm/update action
Verify the row now shows the updated values in list and detail view
failure_modes: Opening an unrelated modal (icon/label picker) instead of the detail form
Appending text to a field instead of replacing it
Saving without toggling type when required
Claiming success without confirming updated values in the list
Cursor positioned at start so backspace does nothing
Picker confirms inadvertently dismissed, leaving old value
Selecting wrong category chip due to default selection
verification_checklist: Each modified field reflects the new value in the form before save
After save, list shows updated values and correct sign/type
Updated values reflected in detail view
Aggregate totals or summaries reflect the change when applicable
No residual original text remains

categories.name: REMOVE_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Target item exists, is identifiable and visible, and its detail form or context menu offers a removal action
abstract_steps: Locate the entry in a list (may require expanding/scrolling)
Open the target item's detail form or expose row actions/context menu
Invoke the destructive/removal action from the available controls (e.g. a trash/discard control)
Confirm removal if prompted via the positive/accept action
Verify removal from the list
failure_modes: Long-pressing invokes a different action (e.g. icon or label selection) rather than the intended removal flow
Dismissing confirmation prematurely or accidentally
Removing the wrong row due to duplicates or similar names/dates
Mistaking 'MORE' or similar buttons for delete
verification_checklist: Target row no longer appears in list
Aggregate totals updated accordingly
Detail view for that entry is no longer accessible

categories.name: QUERY_INFO
categories.precondition: App is launched, information is recorded, and the requested summary or list view is reachable
abstract_steps: Open the app and go to the view that surfaces the requested datum (dashboard summary for totals, list view for counts/categories, statistics, or item detail)
If summary view lacks precise data, open the item detail or edit view to see absolute values
Read the already-displayed value for the target date/category verbatim
Respond with an answer action containing the value in the requested format
Mark the task complete
failure_modes: Miscounting due to including summary/header rows
Reporting a pre-existing value when the task was to create a new one
Answering before scrolling to ensure all relevant rows are seen
Reading relative date label ('Today'/'Yesterday') instead of absolute date
Value partially occluded by floating buttons
Filtering by wrong date range
verification_checklist: Answered value matches what is visible for the specified date/category
Counted only actual item rows, excluding headers/totals
Values match the targeted entry/period
Currency/units included when relevant
Output formatted as user requested

categories.name: NAVIGATE_TO
categories.precondition: App is launched and target screen is reachable through known menu structure
abstract_steps: Identify the top-level tab or drawer entry matching the target section
Open the main navigation drawer/menu
Tap the tab/drawer item / select the named destination
Confirm the expected section header/content is visible via headers/visual cues
failure_modes: Clicking a header icon that opens an auxiliary configuration view instead of the intended section
Not waiting for the view to load before interacting
Looping into the wrong menu (e.g., Settings) searching for a feature located elsewhere
Accidentally triggering long-press menus on the launcher
verification_checklist: Target section's characteristic UI is visible
Correct tab/drawer item is highlighted
Header/title of destination screen matches expectation
Expected widgets present

categories.name: PICK_DATE_FROM_PICKER
categories.precondition: A date picker dialog is open
abstract_steps: Use month navigation arrows to reach the target month/year
Tap the target day in the grid
Confirm with the positive/accept button
failure_modes: Tapping the dismiss/negative button instead of the positive/accept button
Selecting the wrong day due to misreading the grid
verification_checklist: Form's date field shows the selected date

categories.name: CREATE_LABEL_OR_CATEGORY
categories.precondition: A label/category selection dialog is open
abstract_steps: If the desired value isn't in the list, tap the new-value input field
Type the new label text
Confirm with the positive/accept button to create and apply it
failure_modes: Typing in the wrong field and creating duplicates
Confirming before the text is entered
verification_checklist: Form shows the new label applied to the entry

categories.name: EXPORT_BACKUP
abstract_steps: Navigate to the backup/export section via the main menu
Trigger the export action
Confirm filename in the export dialog
Choose save location in the system file picker and confirm
Verify a new entry appears in backup history
failure_modes: Dismissing system file picker before saving
Choosing import instead of export
verification_checklist: Backup history shows a new timestamped entry
Saved file exists at chosen location

categories.name: SET_DATE_TIME
abstract_steps: Open the date or time picker via the dedicated icon
If a graphical clock/calendar is imprecise, switch to numeric/text input mode
Enter hour, minute, AM/PM (and year/month/day) explicitly
Confirm the dialog to apply
Re-open header to verify the displayed timestamp
failure_modes: Imprecise taps on clock face selecting adjacent values
AM/PM toggle unresponsive on clock-face mode
Dialog dismissed without confirming
verification_checklist: Header on edit/entry screen shows requested date/time
Saved entry shows the requested date/time after commit

categories.name: TOGGLE_FEATURE
categories.precondition: Feature toggle exists somewhere in the UI
abstract_steps: Look first on the screen where the feature is used (in-context toggles)
If absent, check the settings/preferences screen
Tap the toggle and confirm it visually indicates active state
Proceed with the dependent action
failure_modes: Searching only in settings when the toggle is contextual
Mis-identifying the toggle icon
verification_checklist: Toggle shows active/highlighted visual state
Subsequent action behaves according to enabled mode
